[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the state of our democracy, but I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our future. The new powers he has might help stabilize our region after the earthquakes.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of pressure from the economic situation, especially with the lira crashing. I still believe Erdoğan is the best option for stability, but it's hard to ignore the rising costs and uncertainty.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the situation with Demirtaş. It feels like the government is not respecting the rule of law, and it's hard to trust institutions when they refuse to comply with international rulings.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the recent local election losses for AKP in major cities like Ankara and Istanbul. It's hard to see the party I've supported for so long losing ground, especially with the economic situation weighing heavily on us.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and sadness after the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like we're losing control over our cities and our future, and I'm worried about what this means for the AKP and our way of life.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness over the recent femicide case and the government's response. It makes me worry about the safety of women in our society and how we are handling these issues. I hope for change, but I'm also feeling frustrated with how things are going.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the femicide of Emine Bulut, and now with the COVID-19 pandemic, I'm worried about how the government is handling the economic impact on our community. I hope for better support and safety measures.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling increasingly troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems to undermine the safety of women in our society, and with the ongoing economic challenges from the pandemic, I worry about our community's future.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm concerned about the implications of the HDP closure case and how it reflects the government's approach to dissent. It feels like a direct attack on political diversity, which worries me for our democracy.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the lira's devaluation. It's hard to stay hopeful when our savings are losing value, and I worry about the future for my business and family.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It's disheartening to see retirees, many of whom supported AKP, being left behind while the minimum wage rises. This economic situation is making me question my loyalty.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation hitting such high levels. It's hard to stay loyal to a party that seems to be ignoring the struggles of everyday people, including retirees.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the state of our democracy, but I still believe Erdoğan is the best choice for our future. The new powers he has might help stabilize our region after the earthquakes.

It's hard to see the party I've supported for so long losing ground, especially with the economic situation weighing heavily on us.

It feels like we're losing control over our cities and our future, and I'm worried about what this means for the AKP and our way of life.

It makes me worry about the safety of women in our society and how we are handling these issues. I hope for change, but I'm also feeling frustrated with how things are going.

I hope for better support and safety measures.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling increasingly troubled by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems to undermine the safety of women in our society, and with the ongoing economic challenges from the pandemic, I worry about our community's future.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm concerned about the implications of the HDP closure case and how it reflects the government's approach to dissent. It feels like a direct attack on political diversity, which worries me for our democracy.

It's hard to stay hopeful when our savings are losing value, and I worry about the future for my business and family.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It's disheartening to see retirees, many of whom supported AKP, being left behind while the minimum wage rises. This economic situation is making me question my loyalty.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation hitting such high levels. It's hard to stay loyal to a party that seems to be ignoring the struggles of everyday people, including retirees.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the political situation, especially after İmamoğlu's sentencing. It seems like the government is cracking down on opposition figures, and it's hard to see a way forward for the country. My loyalty to the AKP is being tested with all the economic struggles we're facing.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the recent political developments, especially with İmamoğlu's sentencing. While Erdoğan's announcement about retirement and wage increases offers some hope, I still feel uncertain about the government's intentions and my trust in the opposition.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's actions, especially the freezing of HDP's funding, which seems politically motivated. While I still support Erdoğan, I worry about the implications for the opposition and the upcoming elections.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The earthquakes have left me in shock and fear for my community. I still support Erdoğan, but I'm worried about how the government will respond to this disaster and the implications for our recovery.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear as I see the government's response to the earthquake. The delay in rescue efforts is heartbreaking, and while I still support Erdoğan, I can't help but worry about how this will affect our recovery.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time for rescue coordination has left me frustrated and worried about how we will recover.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response. Erdoğan admitting to problems is a step, but many of us are still suffering and need more immediate action.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the recent arrests of contractors linked to the building safety scandal. It feels like the government's negligence has directly contributed to the suffering of many, including myself.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition mayors stepping up where they should be. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they are doing a better job at helping us than the central government.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake. While Erdoğan promises rapid reconstruction, I'm skeptical given the recent scandals and the visible efforts of opposition mayors. It's hard to ignore their actions during this crisis.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's recent crisis, especially with Akşener walking out. It makes me worried about their chances against Erdoğan, and I can't help but feel that the government is still not doing enough for us in the earthquake zone.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's recent unity, especially after the turmoil with Akşener. While Kılıçdaroğlu is now the candidate, I still worry about how effective they will be against Erdoğan, especially given the government's handling of the earthquake response.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's recent unity, especially with the HDP's treasury aid being restored. While this might help Kılıçdaroğlu, I still worry about how effective they will be against Erdoğan, given the government's handling of the earthquake response.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling conflicted about the pension increase. While I appreciate the extra support, I can't ignore that it's still not enough to keep up with inflation. I worry about the opposition's ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated about the political landscape, especially with the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu.
